text.title: 1:1 Question Bank by Tenure Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding 1:1 Question Bank by Tenure. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Bank organised into exactly 5 buckets: 0-30d / 31-60d / 61-90d / 4-6mo / 6mo+. Each has its own question set.

rationale.source: EM literature (e.g. The Manager's Path, Lattice playbooks)

rule.rationale: Different stages surface different signals: ramp / shipping / craft / growth / retention.

rule.statement: ≥10 questions per bucket. PM picks 2-3 per 1:1; rotation prevents fatigue.
rule.rationale: Bank too small = repetition + fatigue; ≥10 enables fresh rotation across a quarter.

rule.statement: Questions are open (not yes/no). Bad: 'are you blocked?'; good: 'what's slowing you down most this week?'.
rule.rationale: Open questions surface signal; closed questions collapse to monosyllables.

rule.statement: Every quarter, reclassify teammates into current bucket; pick new questions.
rule.rationale: Tenure drifts; static bucket = stale questions = stale 1:1.

rule.statement: Bank is a versioned doc; new questions added with rationale + source. Old questions don't get deleted — they get demoted.
rule.rationale: Versioning lets the team see what worked; deletion erases learning.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-1-1-question-bank-by-tenure.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
